texto: Dr. Jules Hilbert: Hell Harold, you could just eat nothing but pancakes if you wanted.Harold Crick: What is wrong with you? Hey, I don't want to eat nothing but pancakes, I want to live! I mean, who in their right mind in a choice between pancakes and living chooses pancakes?Dr. Jules Hilbert: Harold, if you pause to think, you'd realize that that answer is inextricably contingent upon the type of life being led... and, of course, the quality of the pancakes.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Zach Helm que provoca limites e escolhas conscientes. Ao questionar “Dr. Jules Hilbert: Hell Harold, you could just eat nothing but pancakes if you wanted.Harold Cric…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, crick, ferrell, com foco em ferrell e fiction.
